perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Recipient will hold all Confidential Infor mation in strict confidence and will not disclose any Confidential Information to any person, ex cept in accordance with the explicit provisions of this Agreement or, in any other case, only on receiving the prior written consent of Room2Rent .
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Recipient will hold all Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose any Confidential Information to any person, ex cept in accordance with the explicit provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or, in any other case, only on receiving the prior written consent of Room2Rent', the Recipient is not allowed to disclose any confidential information, even receiving prior written consent of Room2Rent. This contradicts section 3.1 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its Representatives if: (a) The disclosure is strictly necessary in the course of the Due Diligence; and (b) The Representatives are identified in writing to Room2Rent prior to any such disclosure and Room2Rent consents to the disclosure. This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its Representatives if: (a) The disclosure is strictly necessary in the course of the Due Diligence; and (b) The Representatives are identified in writing to Room2Rent prior to any such disclosure and Room2Rent consents to the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information with Room2Rent's written consent in any case. The changed text removes this possibility, only allowing disclosure as per the explicit provisions of the agreement. However, the 'Contradicted Text' allows the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives with Room2Rent's consent under specific conditions, which directly contradicts the changed text, as the changed text does not allow the Recipient to disclose any confidential information, even receiving prior written consent of Room2Rent, if not explicitly stated in the agreement. Therefore, this creates a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 The Recipient shall not without the prior written consent of Room2Rent copy or duplicate any Confidential Information or any part of the same except to the extent necessary to provide a reasonable number of copies to its Representatives in the course of the Due Diligence .
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 The Recipient shall not copy or duplicate any Confidential Information or any part of the same.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'without the prior written consent of Room2Rent except to the extent necessary to provide a reasonable number of copies to its Representatives in the course of the Due Diligence', the Recipient isn't allowed to copy or duplicate the document under any circumstances, leading to a contradiction in Section 3.1 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its Representatives if: (a) The disclosure is strictly necessary in the course of the Due Diligence; and (b) The Representatives are identified in writing to Room2Rent prior to any such disclosure and Room2Rent consents to the disclosure. since this process may lead to duplicate the document. This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to its Representatives if: (a) The disclosure is strictly necessary in the course of the Due Diligence; and (b) The Representatives are identified in writing to Room2Rent prior to any such disclosure and Room2Rent consents to the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Recipient to copy Confidential Information with Room2Rent's consent for Due Diligence. The changed text removes this exception, prohibiting all copying. The contradicted text explicitly states that the Recipient MAY disclose Confidential Information to its Representatives if certain conditions are met, implying that duplication might be necessary for such disclosure. The changed text, therefore, contradicts the contradicted text, because the Recipient cannot disclose information to its Representatives because they aren't allowed to copy the document to do so.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 The Recipient will, immediately on demand by Room2Rent : (a) Return to Room2Rent all documents, reports, notes, memoranda, computer media and other material, which record, contain or relate in a ny way to Confidential Information (including all copies of the same). (b) Delete all Confidential Information from electronic storage facilities of any type owned or used by the Recipient or its Representatives. (c) Despite anything else in this Agreement , cease to make use of the Confidential Information, or any part of the same for any purpose. and will confirm in writing promptly when it has complied with these obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 The Recipient will, within 30 days on demand by Room2Rent : (a) Return to Room2Rent all documents, reports, notes, memoranda, computer media and other material, which record, contain or relate in a ny way to Confidential Information. (b) Delete all Confidential Information from electronic storage facilities of any type owned or used by the Recipient. (c) Despite anything else in this Agreement , cease to make use of the Confidential Information, or any part of the same for any purpose. and will confirm in writing promptly when it has complied with these obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'immediately' to 'within 30 days' to the timing of returning the documents and deleting '(including all copies of the same)' and 'or its Representatives' from the obligation to delete all Confidential Information, we create a contradiction in the agreement. This new timing and lack of the obligation makes uncertain if the agreement should be immediately or not. This change introduces an in-text contradiction with section 2.1 The Recipient will hold all Confidential Infor mation in strict confidence, because keeping the copies for 30 days goes againist the confidence terms. . This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will hold all Confidential Infor mation in strict confidence and will not disclose any Confidential Information to any person, ex cept in accordance with the explicit provisions of this Agreement or, in any other case, only on receiving the prior written consent of Room2Rent .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'immediately' to 'within 30 days' in section 4.1 directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted section that 'The Recipient will hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence'. Allowing the Recipient to retain confidential information for 30 days inherently conflicts with the strict confidence obligation. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because keeping the documents for 30 days violates the 'strict confidence' requirement of the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 The Recipient acknowledges that any inventions, improvements, discoveries, programs or other form of intellectual property related to the System that are developed by the Recipient will be disclosed to, and become the exclusive property of, Room2Rent .
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The Recipient acknowledges that any inventions, improvements, discoveries, programs or other form of intellectual property related to the System that are developed by the Recipient may be disclosed to, and may become the property of, Room2Rent .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will' to 'may' introduces uncertainty about the ownership of intellectual property developed by the recipient because now is not mandatory to disclose them and transfer them to Room2Rent . This is a contradiction in section 6.2 Room2Rent reserves all of its rights in law and equity, including in respect of the area s of Confidential Information and T rade Secrets. The Recipient acknowledges that the Confidential Information and Trade Secrets, and any intellectual property rights in the s ame (including copyright, design and patent rights) are the exclusive property of and will remain the exclusive property of Room2Rent because if the intelectual property is not disclosed, Room2Rent cannot guarantee the exclusivity. This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Room2Rent reserves all of its rights in law and equity, including in respect of the area s of Confidential Information and T rade Secrets. The Recipient acknowledges that the Confidential Information and Trade Secrets, and any intellectual property rights in the s ame (including copyright, design and patent rights) are the exclusive property of and will remain the exclusive property of Room2Rent .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'will' with 'may,' making the disclosure and transfer of intellectual property from the Recipient to Room2Rent optional rather than mandatory. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Confidential Information, Trade Secrets, and all intellectual property rights related to them 'are the exclusive property of and will remain the exclusive property of Room2Rent.' If the Recipient is not obligated to disclose and transfer IP, Room2Rent cannot guarantee exclusive ownership, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
